steps.title: Keep the phrase attached
steps.content: The sentence says the poem should not be seen only as a product of Brooks's surroundings, considering the wide range of her influences. The word "though" interrupts the sentence to signal contrast, so it needs commas on both sides while keeping "considering" in the same sentence.

steps.title: Reject broken clauses
steps.content: Choice B creates a fragment after the period because "Considering the breadth" does not have an independent clause. Choice C places a semicolon before material that cannot stand alone. Choice D places a semicolon before the participial phrase "considering the breadth," which also cannot stand alone. Choice A keeps the grammar and punctuation intact. The answer is A.
